name: Taiga PM: rootless Podman Compose
description: Rootless Podman Compose stack for Taiga with nginx front door, documented WSL2/Linux workflows, and optional backup/restore scripts.
longDescription: Infrastructure defined in version control for running Taiga without a Docker daemon: Podman Compose definitions, nginx as the HTTP entry (port 8080 by default), and guidance for WSL2 versus native Linux rootless setups. Deployment flow: copy `.env.example`, create persistent data directories, run `podman compose up`, create Django superusers, and optionally load default templates. Shell scripts for backup and restore ship with the stack.
features: Single HTTP entry via nginx reverse proxy
Rootless-first documentation for WSL2 and Linux servers
Data volume layout for postgres, media, static assets, and messaging
Guidance to avoid /mnt/c performance traps on WSL2
highlights: Shows rootless container fluency for long-running collaboration software
challenges: Taiga’s multi-service footprint is sensitive to volume permissions and async workers.
solutions: Document directory prep, env secrets (SECRET_KEY, DB passwords, Rabbit creds), and compose runbooks for first boot.
security: Generate strong secrets for SECRET_KEY, database, and RabbitMQ users
Do not expose port 8080 broadly without TLS termination
